fix: trigger auto-merge on ready_for_review, not just labeled

Copilot coding agent creates a draft PR first, then converts to
ready_for_review when finished. Triggering only on 'labeled' fires
while the PR is still a draft (unmergeable). Now triggers on both

commit.message:
Move Environment.Exit() from ResultConsumer to Main.cs

RunAsync() does return when running on a background thread with
UIApplication.Main() keeping the process alive. Exit explicitly
after RunAsync() completes instead of from the data consumer.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Align animation paper texture with print engine's drawPaperTexture

The animation sheet texture was using a different algorithm than the print
engine, causing visible mismatch between the blank paper laying down and
the finished print. Now mirrors print-engine.js drawPaperTexture exactly:
- Prominent fiber bundles (thick, long, scaled by fiberDensity)
- Fine individual fibers (w*h/400 density scaling)
- Large tonal warmth patches (60-240px radius)
- Unryu long fibers via quadraticCurveTo with matching opacity curves
- Same fiber colors (rgba 185/170/140 and 200/185/155)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reorganize project layout into src/ and tests/ with detailed module split (#39)

repository.name: missionary-lunch-calendar
repository.description: This application aims to solve an issue of creating a calendar to the members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Days Saints to give launch to the missionaries. It stores the proposed days the members are available and easily update for next month keeping the days.

commit.message:
fix(ci): only inject game API script when param is non-empty

Hugo jsonify of empty string produces the JSON literal "" (two chars),
which when stored as a dataset attribute is truthy and caused fetch to
hit ""/leaderboard - a relative URL that 404'd on the Hugo server.

Fix: wrap the injection script in {{ if site.Params.gameApiBase }} so
it is only emitted when the param has a real value. Without the script,
container.dataset.apiBase is undefined → API is '' → early return to
fallback with no fetch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove COPY of non-existent public dir in partner-portal Dockerfile

The partner-portal has no public/ directory; COPY --from=builder fails
with 'not found' when the source path does not exist.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Enforce redirect URI on auth flow (#707)

Fetches all registered redirect URIs for the client ID and fails (A)
there exists any registered URIs and (B) if none of them match.

Also makes ClientID not required for the refresh token grant type to
match what Postman sends, since it can be derived from the token itself.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Skip email and Context7 gracefully when not configured

- Email: skip sending report if SenderAddress, ClientId, ClientSecret,
  or ReportRecipient is missing (log warning instead of failing)
- Context7: log a warning when enabled but API key is missing, or
  debug when explicitly disabled
- Users can now run DemoFresh without setting up Gmail OAuth or
  Context7 and still get console output and PR/delegation functionality

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

NNlib activation function rules (#1043)

* NNlib activation function rules

* tight dispatces

* bump vers

* stability

* loose dispatch to accomodate CUDAArrays

* type stability

* fin

* futureproof fallback, permutation index stability.

* fix

* fix for GPU

* handle Inf instability

* Update ext/MooncakeNNlibExt.jl

Co-authored-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Astitva Aggarwal <84859349+AstitvaAggarwal@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: use arrayify for Adjoint/Transpose in NNlib rrules (#1048)

- Add arrayify methods for Adjoint and Transpose in blas.jl, so
  that arrayify(x, dx) correctly wraps the tangent in adjoint/transpose,
  making dx .+= g equivalent to accumulating g' or transpose(g) into
  the parent tangent via Julia's broadcast setindex! semantics.

- Remove the _accum_fdata! helper and all its dispatch overloads from
  MooncakeNNlibExt. Each pullback now calls arrayify(x) directly on the
  CoDual and destructures: _, dx = arrayify(x); dx .+= g.

- Update tests to exercise arrayify directly for Adjoint/Transpose
  accumulation instead of testing the removed helper.

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Widen SupportedArray to include Adjoint/Transpose (#1049)

* refactor: use arrayify for Adjoint/Transpose in NNlib rrules

- Add arrayify methods for Adjoint and Transpose in blas.jl, so
  that arrayify(x, dx) correctly wraps the tangent in adjoint/transpose,
  making dx .+= g equivalent to accumulating g' or transpose(g) into
  the parent tangent via Julia's broadcast setindex! semantics.

- Remove the _accum_fdata! helper and all its dispatch overloads from
  MooncakeNNlibExt. Each pullback now calls arrayify(x) directly on the
  CoDual and destructures: _, dx = arrayify(x); dx .+= g.

- Update tests to exercise arrayify directly for Adjoint/Transpose
  accumulation instead of testing the removed helper.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Widen SupportedArray to include Adjoint/Transpose of CPU/GPU arrays

   - Redefine SupportedArray{P} as a 1-parameter union covering Array{P},
     AbstractGPUArray{P}, and their Adjoint/Transpose wrappers
   - Inline Union{Array{P,3},AbstractGPUArray{P,3}} for batched_mul (which
     requires N=3 and does not support Adjoint/Transpose)
   - Restore Adjoint/Transpose test cases for softmax, logsoftmax, logsumexp
   - Change all @is_primitive and rrulegit diff signatures from AbstractArray{T}
     to SupportedArray{T}
   - Remove now-unnecessary _xp/xp collect workaround in logsumexp_kw
   - Drop LinearAlgebra: Adjoint, Transpose import (no longer needed)

   Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: avoid PermutedDimsArray type instability in logsumexp kw rrule

On Julia <= 1.11, maximum(x::Adjoint/Transpose; dims, init) routes through
LinearAlgebra.mapreducedim! -> switch_dim12 -> PermutedDimsArray, leaving
type parameters unresolved and causing JET type-stability failures.

Fix: introduce _maximum helper that collects CPU-backed Adjoint/Transpose
wrappers to a plain Array before calling maximum. Dispatches via
x::Tx where Tx<:Union{Adjoint{T,A},Transpose{T,A}} with A<:Array{T}
so the more-specific method is selected for CPU wrappers; all other
SupportedArray types (plain Array, GPU arrays, GPU-backed wrappers) fall
through to the generic method which calls maximum directly.

The entire workaround is version-gated under @static if VERSION < v"1.12"
where the issue was fixed upstream.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Astitva Aggarwal <84859349+AstitvaAggarwal@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace index with id in actions routes (#36842)

This PR migrates the web Actions run/job routes from index-based
`runIndex` or `jobIndex` to database IDs.

**⚠️ BREAKING ⚠️**: Existing saved links/bookmarks that use the old
index-based URLs will no longer resolve after this change.

Improvements of this change:
- Previously, `jobIndex` depended on list order, making it hard to
locate a specific job. Using `jobID` provides stable addressing.
- Web routes now align with API, which already use IDs.
- Behavior is closer to GitHub, which exposes run/job IDs in URLs.
- Provides a cleaner base for future features without relying on list
order.
- #36388 this PR improves the support for reusable workflows. If a job
uses a reusable workflow, it may contain multiple child jobs, which
makes relying on job index to locate a job much more complicated

---------

Signed-off-by: Zettat123 <zettat123@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: initial architecture documentation

Port design documentation from punt-labs/dungeon (pure-prompt v1).
cryptd is the Go game engine daemon that supersedes the Node.js MCP
server and pure-prompt engine in dungeon.

- DESIGN.md: ADRs DES-001 through DES-022; evolved architecture
- docs/architecture.tex/pdf: 19-page technical architecture spec
- docs/plan.md: architecture evolution plan with MCP API and schemas
- docs/testing.md: test pyramid design; fakes; CI configuration
- docs/build-plan.md: 14-milestone development build plan
- docs/review.md: compliance review against punt-kit standards
- prfaq.tex/bib/pdf: PR/FAQ document
- README.md: project overview and documentation index

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(pdf-ocr): chunked PDFs producing empty OCR results (#169)

* Initial plan

* fix(pdf-ocr): save chunks with useObjectStreams:false and validate PDF header

- Use `{ useObjectStreams: false }` when saving PDF chunks with pdf-lib, producing
  PDF 1.4-compatible output (no cross-reference streams) that is more broadly
  supported by OCR engines such as Mistral OCR via OpenRouter.
- Add `%PDF-` magic-byte header validation immediately after each chunk is saved.
  If pdf-lib fails to produce a valid PDF, a clear error is thrown showing the
  chunk number and page range, instead of sending malformed bytes to the OCR API
  and receiving an opaque empty-content response.
- Update existing multi-chunk test: mock save() to return bytes starting with
  `%PDF-1.4` so the new header validation passes, and assert that save() is
  called with `{ useObjectStreams: false }`.
- Add new test: invalid chunk bytes (all zeros, no PDF header) produce a
  descriptive error message identifying which chunk failed.

Co-authored-by: merlinbecker <3381241+merlinbecker@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: merlinbecker <3381241+merlinbecker@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix CI failures: cross-platform path separators, test count, Templates in solution

- ProcessHelpers.builtProjectExecutable: normalize backslash separators via
  Replace('\\', Path.DirectorySeparatorChar) — fixes TypeInitializationException
  crash on macOS where Path.Combine does not treat backslash as separator
- builtProjectExecutable: search both 'Name.exe' and 'Name' (no extension) so
  tests find the executable on Linux/macOS (dotnet build produces no .exe there)
- Hex1bE2ETests: replace @'examples\Counter' literals with Path.Combine('examples','Counter')
  for the same cross-platform reason
- README.md: update test count 2,544 -> 2,551
- SageTUI.slnx: add SageTUI.Templates.fsproj to solution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Bill Easton <bill.easton@elastic.co>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix settings code blocks using Bootstrap red instead of theme colors (#331)

## Problem
The \<code>\ elements inside \.section-desc\ in the Settings page (e.g.
\origin/main\, \~/.polypilot/plugins/\, \plugin.json\) were inheriting
Bootstrap's \--bs-code-color\ (\#d63384\, pinkish-red) because no
explicit \color\ was set in the CSS. This was jarring and hard to read
in Dark mode.

## Root Cause
Bootstrap sets \code { color: var(--bs-code-color); }\ globally with
\--bs-code-color: #d63384\. The chat output had already been fixed to
override this with \ ar(--text-bright)\ in \ChatMessageList.razor.css\,
but \.section-desc code\ in \Settings.razor.css\ only had \ ackground\
and \padding\ — no color override.

## Fix
Added \color: var(--text-bright)\ and \ order: 1px solid
var(--border-subtle)\ to \.section-desc code\ in \Settings.razor.css\,
matching the approach used in \ChatMessageList.razor.css\ for markdown
code blocks.

## Tests
- 7 new unit tests in \SettingsCodeBlockThemeTests.cs\ verifying theme
variables are used and Bootstrap red is not present
- 1 new UI scenario in \mode-switch-scenarios.json\ to verify computed
styles at runtime

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Use UIApplication.Main() for proper UIKit run loop

Run MSTest on a background thread from AppDelegate.FinishedLaunching.
This prevents iOS watchdog kills during long test runs by maintaining
a proper UIKit run loop, without the complexity of XCTest integration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: iOSDotNetTest
repository.description: A prototype iOS "project template" for `dotnet test`

commit.message:
Make HasUsedToolsThisTurn volatile for ARM memory model safety (#344)

## Problem

`HasUsedToolsThisTurn` on `SessionState` is read by the watchdog timer
thread and SDK background threads while being written from the UI thread
and SDK event handlers. It had **inconsistent synchronization**: 5 sites
used `Volatile.Read/Write` while 16 used plain access.

On ARM (iOS/Android), plain writes from the UI thread may not be
immediately visible to background thread reads. This is a theoretical
data race that could cause the watchdog to use incorrect timeouts (120s
vs 600s).

Identified during code review of PR #342 (INV-7 from the
processing-state-safety skill).

## Fix

- Declare the field `volatile bool` — all reads/writes automatically
have correct memory barriers
- Remove the 5 now-redundant `Volatile.Read/Write` calls (would generate
CS0420 warnings)
- Remove a duplicate write in the reconnect path (line ~2613 was
redundant with ~2639)
- Replace the `HasUsedToolsThisTurn_VolatileConsistency` test with
`HasUsedToolsThisTurn_IsDeclaredVolatile` that uses reflection to verify
the volatile modifier

## Testing

All 2401 tests pass. No CS0420 warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: mobile-responsive header and summary tiles (#52)

* feat: mobile-responsive layout for header and summary tiles

Dashboard header:
- Restructure to a two-row layout on small screens: title + action
  buttons always visible, metadata (Updated / Signed in as) hidden
  below sm breakpoint to prevent cramping on 375px viewports
- Reduce gap between action buttons from gap-4 to gap-2

SummaryTiles:
- Change loading and live tile grids from grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2
  to grid-cols-2 — tiles are always shown 2-up on mobile (not stacked
  single-column) giving a compact, scannable layout on phones
- Stack the Overall Compliance bar vertically on mobile (flex-col
  sm:flex-row) so the status breakdown doesn't overflow narrow screens
- Add flex-wrap to the status breakdown and the expiring-soon warning
  bar so entries wrap gracefully rather than truncating

All 7 table components already had overflow-x-auto — no changes needed.
73/73 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
